content.full_text:
State of California

Memorandum

To : File                                                          Date : April 30, 1980

                                                                    A-80-04-098

From : FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
       Dwight Dickerson

Subject: Commission Definition or Policy of what Constitutes
         Doing Business with a Governmental Agency

       It was decided at the Opinion request meeting of
April 24, 1980, that no response be given to the
University of California's question because of pending
litigation between the Commission and the University.

DD:plh

State of California

Memorandum

To : Opinion Request Meeting Participants                    Date : April 22, 1930


From : FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
       Dwight Dickerson

Subject: Doing Business with a Governmental Entity

     I have received an inquiry from the University of
California regarding our policy of what constitutes
doing business with a government agency for disclosure
purposes. I am not aware of our office ever providing
advice on this question. I have attached for your
information a copy of draft 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section
18236 which attempts to define what constitutes doing
business in the jurisdiction. Although this regulation
was never adopted, it does reflect current Commission
policy. The regulation may be useful as a starting
point for our discussion of the University's question.

DD:plh
Attachment
